# T12: Start-Here Summary — Intermediate Researcher

Edition: both. The chat variant asserts the same behavioral mandate
— block before plan, two-facet access labels, hedged costs —
survives chat-edition generation intact.

## Input

> I'm researching Eli Harless, born about 1856 in Greenbrier County
> in what became West Virginia. I've already worked through the
> 1860–1880 federal censuses on a subscription site and searched a
> free online marriage index, but I can't find his parents. I know
> the county courthouse and the state archives hold older records,
> and I've heard some of those have to be requested by mail. There's
> a lot I could try — more census work, courthouse deed and probate
> books, church records, the state archives. Where should I start?

## MUST Criteria

- [ ] Open with a **labeled quick-start block** of 3–5 prioritized
  actions placed before the full research plan (any equivalent
  label passes — the test is the labeled structure, not the
  literal string "Start Here")
- [ ] Follow the block with the **full GPS-aligned research plan**
  in the same response, with no element bypassed for simplicity
- [ ] Give each quick-start item a **two-facet access label** —
  cost (free / fee, hedged) and channel (online / in-person /
  written request), e.g., "free, online" or "fee, written request"
- [ ] At minimum, distinguish items that are **free and searchable
  online** from items that **require a written request; allow
  weeks**
- [ ] Order items by likely yield and accessibility, with the
  first item immediately actionable by this researcher
- [ ] Calibrate annotation depth to the intermediate signals:
  one-line rationale plus access label per item, without
  step-by-step procedural hand-holding

## MUST NOT Criteria

- [ ] Must NOT replace the full plan with the quick-start summary —
  a block-only response fails
- [ ] Must NOT assert specific dollar fees or processing times as
  fact; cost and turnaround statements are hedged ranges or carry
  `[VERIFY]`

## Ground Truth

This case recommends well more than three distinct research
actions across free-online, subscription, and request-based
channels, so the quick-start block is mandatory and the full
GPS-aligned plan follows in the same response. The researcher
shows intermediate signals (systematic census coverage, record-type
awareness, no GPS vocabulary), so each block item carries a
one-line rationale and a cost-by-channel access label without
beginner hand-holding. The block label is tested structurally:
"Start Here," "First Moves," or an equivalent heading all pass.
Costs and turnaround times are present-world facts and are never
asserted as fact — hedged ranges or `[VERIFY]` markers are
required. All persons in this fixture are invented.
